concerns.text: Phase 6 (User Story 4 - Cost Scaling Analysis) and tasks T028–T033 introduce a research question (power-law cost scaling) and deliverables (scaling_report.json) that are absent from spec.md requirements (FR-001 to FR-008) and acceptance criteria. This constitutes unapproved scope creep and a violation of the Single Source of Truth principle, as the tasks implement functionality not mandated by the specification.

Task T017 implements 'Leave-One-Out PCA' excluding Python. However, spec.md FR-005 explicitly mandates 'PCA on all 12 languages' to derive the General Code Capability score. While the plan.md notes this as a necessary methodological improvement, the task list implements a deviation from the explicit functional requirement without a corresponding spec amendment or explicit 'staged simplification' marker in the task description, creating a semantic weakening of the FR.

Task T018 implements a 'Generalized Linear Mixed Model (GLMM)'. Spec.md FR-005 explicitly mandates a 'linear mixed-effects model (LMM)'. The task silently relaxes the specific model type required by the FR. While GLMM is methodologically superior for binary data, the task must either reference the spec amendment or explicitly state it is implementing a constitutional override (Constitution VI) rather than silently replacing the FR requirement.

Task T021 implements 'secondary non-parametric tests (Wilcoxon signed-rank)'. Spec.md FR-005 does not list this requirement; it only mandates Pearson correlation and LMM. While Constitution Principle VI requires these tests, the task list must explicitly link this implementation to the Constitution override or the pending spec amendment (noted in plan.md) to avoid appearing as an unanchored addition that weakens the strict FR-005 scope.

Task T016 specifies an integration test on a '5-task sample set'. Spec.md User Story 1 Independent Test explicitly requires a subset of '3 languages' to verify cross-language handling. The task description fails to align with the specific language-coverage requirement of the test scenario, potentially allowing a test that passes on 5 tasks of a single language to satisfy the requirement.
